questions.question: What does the acronym FPGA stand for in the context of digital electronics?
options: Field-Programmable Gate Array
Fast Processing Global Architecture
Fully Parallel Gate Accelerator
Frequency Protocol Generated Area
Flexible Peripheral Graphics Adapter

questions.question: During the logic synthesis stage of digital IC design, what is the primary objective of "logic optimization"?
options: To convert the gate-level netlist back into an RTL description for verification.
To place and route standard cells on the silicon die.
To reduce the gate count, improve timing performance, and minimize power consumption of the design.
To verify the functionality of the design through extensive simulations.
To generate the test patterns for Design for Testability (DFT).

questions.question: In modern complex System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, what is the primary motivation for adopting a Network-on-Chip (NoC) architecture instead of traditional bus-based interconnects?
options: To simplify the process of analog circuit integration.
To reduce the overall manufacturing cost of the chip.
To improve scalability, performance, and power efficiency for communication between numerous IP blocks.
To eliminate the need for memory controllers within the SoC.
To strictly enforce sequential consistency across all processing elements.

questions.question: What is the fundamental advantage that formal verification techniques (e.g., model checking, equivalence checking) offer over traditional simulation-based verification in digital IC design?
options: Formal verification can run significantly faster than simulation, especially for large designs.
Formal verification provides exhaustive proof of correctness across all possible input sequences and states, unlike simulation which only checks observed scenarios.
Formal verification requires significantly less computational resources and memory compared to simulation.
Formal verification is primarily used for analog circuit verification, not digital.
Formal verification is easier to set up and debug for complex designs than simulation.

questions.question: In high-performance computer architectures, what is the primary goal of "speculative execution"?
options: To reduce the physical size of the processor by eliminating unnecessary components.
To pre-fetch data from main memory into cache even if it's not immediately needed.
To improve instruction-level parallelism and hide latency by executing instructions before it's certain they are required, based on predictions.
To ensure that all memory operations strictly adhere to sequential consistency.
To provide a secure sandbox environment for untrusted code execution.
